text: T mobile needs to have people who are more educated and know the job and people who speak good english and i can understand i been with you all 15 years the only reason is bc. i hate change but you really stink i would say other words but i am godly the service the prices is so terrible will I recommend no  i quality of this business is declining period  and to me your not even one star i am so mad 😡 with all this
Horrible customer service I will not recommend T-Mobile . They miss lead  customers

We just got to new I phone 2 months ago. The sales lady said it would be $87 for the senior plan. We ask several times with everything that’s what the bill would be. So we called this week because of some credits and the customer service lady said it’s $138 per month. The sales lady Lied to us!!!!
If I knew this I would have Not changed!!

This is really crap… I told the lady to go back and listen to the recording 
Poor Service and LIERS!!
Beware

I have been a very loyal customer now for several years. I pay my bill and also included on that bill I pay for 2 different types of insurance coverage. I was avised when opening the account NO MATTER WHAT HAPPENS TO THE PHONE, YOU WILL BE ABLE TO HAVE IT REPLACED OR UPGRADED EITHR FREE OF CHARGE OR FOR THE $100 DEDUCTIBLE. I then spent over 4.5 hours, yes, over four and a half hours on the phone with Sprint and Assurant. The phone I need to replace I have had for over 2 years and they still expected me to pay $00 for the old phone and then an additional $243 for the new replacement phone. Assurant told me due the the issue being a manufacturers defect, then Sprint tells me no matter what the reason it is on Assurant because that is what insurance is for. I thought the same, but I was wrong. Four and a half hours later and after being hung up on 5 different times because they would say, okay let me update your account and I would hear hold music for maybe 2- minutes the bam hung up on. Call back and of course none of the reps I spoke to left any note on my account about the info., Sprint finally agreed to replace the defective iphone 11with another iphon 11 but I would habe to pay $240 for the replacement. There is so much more detail to this review, but not enough time or space to write it all out.

I paid for service I did not receive.  They did not tell me that the nearest cell tower was 2 miles away, and therefore the reception would be bad.  They promised a booster that they never sent.  They did not reduce bills even when I showed them pics of various failed calls etc, and when I finally left after 3 months of suffering, they still charged me for service I simply did not receive.  Lesson learned.
horrible coverage and customer service thought after buying sprint it would have improved ,,,it did NOT
The worst...i might have daid this already

After over 10 years with this lousy company (Sprint), I finally gave up and left the company (now T-Mobile) as a customer. If they can't hire competent people to deal with customer service I don't trust them with 5G and you shouldn't either. Check out my reports on Bitchute and Rumble so you can hear for yourself how they lie to their customers. It's worth a listen so you can realize that they don't care about customers. Search the titles: "5G Side Effects TMobile Affirmative Action Hires Outsourcing Kill Public Trust Endanger Customers", "5G Radiation Danger T-Mobile Hires I diots To Roll Out Weapon System Telecommunications Infrastructure", "5G Danger Cancer Cellular DNA Damage Lower Fertility Libido T-Mobile Outsources Itself Into S tupidity", "5G Danger Brain Cancer Tumors Heart Attack Memory Loss T-Mobile Force Deadly Untested 5G On Customers".

his company sucks they had a data breach. Millions of personal information was stolen. License social security numbers credit card numbers. My information was stolen everything I mean all my info was taken. I was alerted by experian that all my info was stolen. They informed me it was from T-Mobile. They even emailed me the proof. I contact t mobil they told me they knew about. They also told me that basically I was on my own. They could not do absolutely anything for me. I spoke with different people in company and basically they all told me nicely to go F myself. I would not recommend this company to no one it all about their money. They do not care for anyone. Please go to another cell phone company that would not tell you to f yourself.

Our account has been hacked twice and thousands of dollars in new phones and new phone lines, adjustments to our plan that we did not make have been allowed twice on our account! This company sucks and their service sucks. Changing immediately. If I could give them zero stars I would

I called to ask TMobile why they took the payment from my account without my knowledge or consent after they bought out Sprint. Simple question really right? First off the account is under my husbands cellphone but I have that access number no worries…I thought. It’s 5am when I called so I can speak to someone she decided for whatever hair curl went wrong to talk to my husband who is sleeping and she’s insistent of me doing so maybe because she couldn’t help? But that came later. I woke my husband up then he gives the code etc. then this lady texted him 4 more numbers to repeat back to her just to make sure I guess it’s him. Okay that’s the first wonderful experience of being bent over the table. I then ask why was I billed and paid on the 28th then 5 days later you took out another payment can you explain that please. She gives me the reason with nonsense included and asks for more information and says I see you made 2 payments but this is now TMobile we don’t can’t see Sprint accounts and they have their own customer service I said so you just said you saw both payments yes, she said yes, so I then asked her if you are 2 different companies under one name can you access my old account and tell me if I have a credit. She said I don’t have access to that account? I then reminded her of what she told me less than 5  minutes earlier and said you can’t see my account can you? Sheepishly she says no, so you lied to me then didn’t you? **silence** you lied didn’t you? **silence** now I’m hot so she says let me get sprint customer service the best service guy he can help you. Now I’m on a three way call and Jesse (NS60087) asks for verification so I’m thinking okay TMobile lady, said her name was Karen, can handle that she just spoke to a half awake man. Jesse says no I need his pin or answer to question of childhood friend okay I have no code but Karen does Jesse doesn’t want it from Karen so I give the name of childhood friend. My husband is Spanish so it’s Miguel or Michael he says nope that’s not right I need your husband. He is now shaving I have to get him he is a little hot too his childhood friend name oh sure Mike. Okay last I checked Mike, Michael and Miguel same yes? These people are making us lose our temper at 5:25am now so then when Jesse is done with his session of upset off the customer my husband is transferred during call to someone else entirely 3rd person now. So the question was why did we get charged twice in 5 days because our account numbers changed and that was the golden answer that would have taken 3 minutes if Karen hadn’t lied to me about what she could or couldn’t see and Jesse didn’t mess with us even more with Mike not being Michael or Miguel. Seriously?? Oh and “Karen” hung up when I asked for her ID number and Jesse is the one that said her name was Karen. Could’ve been fixed with transfer to Sprint you have two accounts merging that’s why easy as 123. Nope they didn’t have common sense I think on the application for customer service says common sense check NO also please feel free to lie to your customer too.

I've had T-Mobile for 3 Years and had many dropped calls during this time in Hanover Township, Pa. I called too many times to complained about them. They said it was my phone. But they failed recognize that I have 4 phones on the same plan. It was never tended to. In the last 5 months my automatic withdrawal got erased somehow. So my monthly special rate was no more and they wouldn't except my VISA as payment. I called Visa about this and they said it was T-Mobile. So I decided to switch to AT&T and now T-Mobile won't unlock 3 of my phones until a paid the remainder of my bill.  This is the thanks  I get for over 3 years I've been a faithful customer.

I have T-Mobile Home Internet.  I am satisfied with the router; however, I am not satisfied with their gimmicks. This is why I gave them such a low rating. I was told I would get a free tablet with the router. I told them I don't want a tablet; they said it's free and comes with the router. Let me warn you; it is not free. I had no choice because the tablet was packed with the router.
When I received the first invoice, the cost of the router was increased by $5 to use the tablet on their network and $9 for insurance for the tablet. I had many phone calls with them about this to no avail.  I cancelled the monthly cost for the tablet insurance and $5 use.  The bottom line is I now have a tablet I don't need and will not use and I have to pay T-Mobile $7 per month to buy MY FREE TABLET for 24 months.  In other words, T-Mobile evaluates the cost of MY FREE TABLET to be $168. I offered to return the brand new, unused tablet to them but T-Mobile said if I return it to them, they will still charge me $7 per month. Something seems a bit unfair here.

Frank Bella

What does the T mean in T-Mobile = T for TRASH 

T- Mobile has turned out to be the biggest mistake I have ever made with a cell phone company. 
I am NOW being charged $153.99 for canceling my service after fraud accrued at one of the stores with a promise of a BOGO (Buy One Get One) to get NEW PHONES. I got one phone but not the other. So I canceled the T Mobile account after 2 years. And then switched over to Verizon.
So Glad Verizon gave me the BOGO and then some extras. 

T Mobile has the worst customer service; they had a hard time understanding and speaking the English language. I placed many calls to customer service at T-Mobile and still not able to fix the  billing what so ever. FINIALLY when I got through to a supervisor and I thought it was cleared up and ZEROED out…. WELL NOT so fast…. I just got another BILL for $153.99. PLUS $35.00 for disputing the charge.... WAT TO GO Trash Mobile. 

I will now resort to using social media to bash the company anyway I can. T-Mobile = TRASH MOBILE until the $153.99 is canceled from my account. The LIES, The Fraud and the Confusing 
My account # is 972097728
#TMOBILESUCKS #TrashMobile

Was promised by one of their live "experts" that my new phone would be free with a trade in. Failed to mention to me that I actually did not qualify for the free trade in. Found out about a month after purchasing the phone. Talked to them and essentially told me I had to pay for it anyways. That's not even mentioning the activation of the phone was like pulling teeth from a lion. No activation kit came with the phone when I got it in the mail. Chatted with another "expert" and they told me they had activated the phone and it would be ready in 4 hours. 2 days go by and the phone is not activated and I'm going back and forth with them and they can't seem to figure out how to activate my phone. Finally, I came up with the solution of grabbing the sim card from my old phone and putting it in the new one. What do I even need them for? Very disappointed.

Wanted to get away from AT&T and tried to switch to T-Mobile. What a nightmare. Problem after problem. They wanted to start my billing date the day I placed an order. I called them up and asked how can you bill me when I haven’t even received a SIM card from you yet. They said, “well you have service from us, you have an account.” And I told them I could not make a call or anything with their service till my SIM card arrived. They persisted and I cancelled my order. Took a lot of correspondence and effort to let them understand I was not paying for their service since I never used their service. They finally sent  credit for start up fees and cancelled the 1st months service bill.

If I could, I would give it 0 stars. 
An absolute scam for a phone service. I recently changed over to Verizon due to the unexpected high bill they gave up for lines that was always on the account. And they did not give a reason why the bill increased. Bill was raised from $430.00 to over $759.00 in a MONTH. We removed all of the extra lines on there with the help of customer service, only for the bill to increase because of "mid-cycle changes". They charged us another $40.00 per change that was made to the account. Also, customer service do nothing but give you empty promises of what they can do to help you. DO not take anything they say seriously, as they will say anything to get you off of the line. 
DO not get them as a phone service, they will also try to sneak in small charges onto your account without you noticing. 

Did I mention that I've had this service for over 11 years? So much for trying to keep loyal customers.
I have the UNLIMITED plan with Tmobile. But once I use 50GB of data they restrict during peak hours my data. I day trade stocks and this is bad to have.

This review is based on my current experience. Part I…. To make a long story short… I receive voicemails hours to days after they are left on my voicemail. Service is extremely spotty. The Company offered, CellSpot, was useless and returned to T-Mobile. Approximately four times now, I had to call T-Mobile and ask them the reason I do not receive password reset codes via text messaging or phone calls from businesses (which are directed right to my voicemail without ringing my phone). Each time I was told there was a block on my line. Again, I removed the block four times. However, somehow, the block is always put back on my line. I received four different answers as to the reason this happens. Recently traveled to upstate New York where there is virtually no T-Mobile service at all, whatsoever! Each time I contact t-Mobile it takes over an hour to bring the techs up to speed. Afterwards, everything remains the same. Nothing ever changes. The fact that I was grandfathered into a low cost plan is the only reason I stay with T-mobile. However, it looks as if others are catching up. One big change that T-mobile made, which I  am extremely disappointed with, is their online chat…. they got rid of it! My Apple Watch has cell service. However, it rarely ever works. To be continued…

I have had t/mobile for over 30yrs, even though their records show about 18yr, it's wrong. I am about to switch to AT&T. When we first moved to our house 22yrs ago the reception was great! About 5yrs of living here it started to go downhill. I have time after time talked to t/mobile about the reception here in this area getting worse and nothing has been done about it. I never get an answer back all I know it's getting worse. We are having missed calls and when  we try to send texts out, it just sits there going in circles until it says "Failed to send." Then we have to turn off and restart our phones for it to send the text. My wife and I are sick of this "no service" from T/mobile apparently they don't care about all their customers service! I hate to break this relationship up but I may have to if nothing improves. My coworkers and friends that have AT&T in this area have great reception! If you go to our church which is about a mile away and stand outside, forget about inside, you get no reception, you have to connect to the wifi of the church to make a call or answer one. I hope they send me another survey and they will not get 10's like I used to give them. This is happening with both of our phones so it's the system. Something they did a while back & don't want to fix it. My advise, see how others in your area get T/mobile reception before you go to them. I just may be leaving because I need a phone that gets connected to it's mobile company. Let's see if they even read this, I doubt it, they've become too big for their customers. If I knew there was an investigative reporter that would be interested I would tell them about it. Maybe the PSC may be interested.

WARNING- Cell phone sales people are as crooked as car sales men. I should have stayed with pre-paid services!! I have had nothing but lies and misrepresentation from THEIR own employees, about everything from prices, services, repairs and especially UPGRADES!! IF YOU DECIDE TO PURSUE T-MOBILE- MAKE THEM PROVE IN WRITING EVERYTHING THEY SAY!! 
Going back to pre paid with BOOST. T-mobile Tuesdays is a joke also.

I just want to start off by saying I absolutely HATE T-mobile. I had been with sprint and everything was fine up until there were all the changes and t-mobile came in. I have such an issue with them and they make it complicated for me to even cancel my watch service that I did not even sign up for with the migration happened. I HATE them with a passion. Please save yourself the trouble and stick to whatever phone company you have. T-mobile is so crooked and incompetent.

If i could give 0 stars i would. I signed up for their home internet. Their salesman was of course really nice, American, easy to talk to. Told me it was the best internet and the speeds were 5g so it would be great. He sold it fantastically. Well they wanted to run a credit check. I was taken back by that as this is just internet. Why waste a credit pull for that. I proceeded anyway. Approved and he tells me they can do quick shipment for a little extra. I declined as i wasnt in a hurry. Well shipment came in 3 days anyway. I was on the road at the time but that weekend i was back home. I installed the modem and low and behold. No signal. So i moved the modem to about 10 different locations in my home. Including the garage. No signal or very low signal. I called customer support and was on the phone for almost 2 hours with them. Trying all sorts of things. It just didnt work out. The internet signal was way too low to connect. I told them that weve tried but i wouldnt be able to keep this service as it doesnt work. I received a ups return label on Feb 27th. It was a Sunday so i didnt return the box until later in the week.  I received a bill for the full amount and gave them a call on the 6th. That was a hassle in itself. I was told everything was taken care of and no worries and that i wouldnt owe anything. Well....... another bill came. So i called again. Every call was to a person working out of their home over seas. I have no problem with foreigners but it seems theres a miscommunication going on or T Mobile just dont give a crap. All of their complaints go to foreigners but sales go to Americans. Anyway.... After speaking with them i was assured it was taken care of. About a week later i have another bill saying payment is due now blah blah blah. I called them again and of course it goes over seas again. I can actually hear kids in the background. I told them i wanted something in writing sent to me email. Since my credit was pulled i was afraid this bill will be reported on my credit. So if it did i have some type of proof that i wasnt suppose to be billed. Of course the lady couldnt email me anything but assured me its taken care of and in the notes. That i had to wait for the billing cycle to process. We now its the 30th of March. I received another letter saying i owe them still. GGGRRRR!!!! I call again and go through the long process of them finding my acount. Being put on hold etc etc. This time the guy tells me they are waiting on the warehouse to scan my equipment so they know its been returned. I told him thats crap because i received an email when the item was returned. So how can i have confirmation but you dont. Let me speak to a higher up, manager, supervisor. Because this issue is just going to continue on. He puts me on hold and i waited about 10 min on hold. Yes i timed it. The same guy gets on the phone and says hello. I say hello and then they hung up the phone. Sol i searched for the corporate number and called them. They transferred me right back to the foreigners. Same process all over again, Screw T-Mobile and anyone who supports them. They are a crap company that i would never do business with. The nerve of them to ask me to transfer my Verizon cell service over to them. My issue is still not resolved.

I been with this company for 6 years I am so tired of this phone company They miss my bill up over charge me I placed two orders they send one out then I keep calling for the other one the workers keep lying to me about my other they told me their wear house has to send it then my bank card then my address but yet I got one order I placed on the same today this is the worse phone company t Mobile worse I can not wait to leave this company

I went in the other day to purchase a new phone from T-Mobile at Rialto location. Let me say the experience went so well. Stephany was the one who helped me the whole way through. I was going back and forth undecided of which phone to choose. I appreciated that she had great patience with me. And I can say I am very happy of my choice. Thank You Stephany- Thank You T-Mobile.  THXS Robt.

Remember being young and you were at a party in a field, or somewhere way out off some dirt road. Its a huge party and everyone is thrashing out, everyone is happy and everything is groovy. Then you meet some one that you think is the most raddest person ever. And they also are digging on you. You think it's going to be a epic night.. Then you hear a slight commotion,...you don't think much of it and go about throwing game and feeling the night. Then you notice people running in every direction. The cat in the Phish shirt and smells like patchouli is running a throwing stuff out of his pockets every where. The weird dude that showed up with a snake and is wearing leather pants and a Iron maiden shirt is trying to run...But dude has tight leather pants on and has a 13 ft snake in tow so he is more really just shuffling quickly and looks like he is so doing the hustle. You look over and notice the really spaced out couple that have been playing with glow sticks and staring at a tree leaf all night that they say resemble "A goat driving a boat"... I don't see the resemblance but ok? Then you notice what seems to be flash lights coming towards you off in the distance as the commotion and people trying to figure out what to do is continuing. You then realize it's the cops that have showed up. You think to yourself well no point in running I have been here to long and my car is blocked in anyway. I will just try and talk to the cops. Maybe since we are the people that didn't run they will be cool,...Oh...wait...great the person of my dreams booked up now to and I think I see??..Is that a snake trying to wrap around her leg to??? Well that sucks but here come the cops. Good evening officers,fine night huh? The cops seem cool they don't seem to want to bust ya for anything. They tell you you can go and everything is fine they aren't going to charge you with anything....You go to your car and eventually after a lot of 3 point turns and also having to deal with the weird dazed out couple asking if you have seen a goat in a truck pulling a boat? Then a week later after not even thinking about the party or the cops, going about your life. You get the mail and find a letter that says you owe a fine of 600 dollars and you need to pay another 500 fine for parking in a illegal area . You think wow even though I stayed and didn't run and make the cops chase me all over you get a fine,but everyone that ran got away are completely fine and don't have any big fines to pay...... That's what it's like ever since T-Mobile merged with Sprint. I have been at the sprint party for years!! Never had any issues. Now the T-Mobile cops show up and the party is over. I and my wife have had bill issues we can't get resolved because customer service is worthless. They tell you the issue is resolved but it never is. Even after calling multiple times to get it resolved. Now after I paid off my phone that was top of the line when I got it and it still works great not a scratch on it. I'm being forced to get another phone. I love my phone. I don't need or want another phone or the payment on my bill. But I either get a payment for a good phone,or take the seriously POS phone that is no where near as good as good a  phone as mine,that they give out for free to make it look like they are trying to help people out. Its the biggest scam and I seriously can't believe they are getting away with it. And good luck trying to call anyone and talk to them about it lol.... They will either play the put you on hold game,or talk of the script they are gave to confuse and frustrated people till they hang up. Transfer you 10 times make you explain the same thing over and over to the next transfer. And this is a good one... When they know you have them and they are definitely in the wrong. Put you on hold for 20 minutes and then,..Oh the line gets disconnected lol. They have the "Manager will call you back the next day" game to lol. I have seriously been a Sprint customer since the Nextel days. And I have never had any problems that weren't easily solved. T-Mobile shows up and ever since. The party is busted up... Good thing my contract is over. Although all the phone companies are crooked. But If I'm going to be forced to buy a new phone I'm not giving my money to what T-Mobile has put me through and from what I can plainly see a lot of other people have gone through. It just blows my mind that they can get away with what they do....

I believed all the hype about T-Mobile after being a Verizon customer my entire life and I decided to give them a shot, false advertising about any trade in of any Samsung Galaxy phone for the new 22 I brought 3 different phone's into the store and they told ne neither one of them was eligible so whats the purpose of saying any year any model any condition??? I will not be extended my contract with T-Mobile more like T-Garbage

When I upgraded two iPhones in my group to the iPhone13 and airpods, I was told the airpods are free. That was all over tv commercials as well. Now t-mobile is charging me for them saying that they used the wrong promo number. I ordered the promotion on the phone so, even if what they are saying is true, it is their fault that I am paying for. Be careful when you believe t-mobile's promotions. I will switch to a more truthful company as soon as I can.

I changed my cell service to T-mobile from Verizon on February 23. My first bill was $50 for service for January 23 to February 22, before I had service with T-mobile. I was told that I just didn't understand the bill. I was billed again for $51.46 on March 23, 29 days after my service started. I went back to Verizon on March 25. T-mobile says I have to pay for the whole month because it is  their policy. I paid $101.46 for  31 days service. Shame on T-mobile.

Worst customer service ever and they quote one price then hit you with a higher bill. Regret leaving Verizon even though they are a bit more.  11 years with them never had one issue with customer service!!

I dud call customer service tonight , and expend time to ask about new promotions, it was fine , but the service representative was no nice , because I did ask different questions and he star to getting nasty and unpolite and I was trying to purchase two phones on line and suddenly, he hang up the phone conversation on me , that is the problem with some representatives from tmobil overseas , they are not patience and they don't care abd this give a bad name to your company !!, they should hire better people and more educated ones and more professionals !!!!!!

Their cell service has been fine but the WiFi is a completely different story. When it is working it’s outstanding but it is totally unreliable. No idea if they oversold the home WiFi with enough bandwidth or what but if you want reliable home WiFi go somewhere else.

I am a prior sprint transferee to Tmobile. I am highly dissatisfied with the inability to read my voicemail when it's an option I've paid for and had no issues with, with Sprint. Also, why do I lose internet when my service goes to roam and I'm in the hotspot mode tethered to my computer? This has cause a lot of issue with my work. these are incredible concerns for me. For as much as Tmobile was and is hyped up, I'm not experiencing it. The swithing of sim cards has caused me a lot of issue.
I've been with T-Mobile for over a year very reliable I love their company

edits.text: I've had T-Mobile for over a year I've never had a service any better in my life just to let y'all know so many other providers are not

text:
Have been a customer since 2001 (nextel), then sprint. I have never had a issue and if I did it was taken care of that day. Then T-Mobile comes in. I upgradeds two of my phones and in turn i was to return one to them. Box comes in I send it out. I am the one that just pays the bill and never really looks it over (my mistake). I realize that T-Mobile is the highest utility bill I am paying. I start looking over the bill and there it is. They are still charging me the additional 58.34 a month lease and full price for the new phone that was supposed to be half price. I called the company and explained I waned the charges removed and the credit of 350.04 for the last six months. 2 hours later they said a manger would call me back with in 24 hours. NO CALL BACK! I gave it a week and called back and now after 3 1/2 hours on the phone a person from retention CLAIMS she has given me the credit and removed the monthly charges and would take effect with in 48 hours This was on 3/21/22. Check the account on 3/25/22 and to my surprise NO CREDIT! Call back again and they want me to explain the situation again. Now I get what they call a retention supervisor (Joke) They only want to credit me half of what they owe me and not remove the monthly additional fees because they claim they have not received the phone I sent them. They want me to buy out the 2 contracts to stop the charges. Needless to say this call only took a hour because I will be done with T-Mobile on 4/18/22. I never write a review but the way T-Mobiles customer service has handled this situation with being a customer for over 20 years people need to know. Also if you are buying a phone on a month to month basis you must buy that phone out before canceling your service, you can not just return it (small print). Wish there was a 0 star rating!
P.S. when ever you talk to there customer service GET A CLAIM NUMBER! W

Hate to say it but I have been with Sprint for 21 years. It’s been pretty bad. Now with the t-mobile merger I thought it might get better how dumb of me. It’s worse. They lie (literally) the workers always trying to upsell. Workers drop calls make you start over or they have hung up. It’s so bad and like an idiot I’ve been dealing with the Same BS 21 years

Canceled T Mobile internet because of internet interruptions no matter where I put the modem.  They said they have it in my area and that it would handle my working from home; which was wrong. It could lose someone their job. Well after I canceled it and returned all the equipment I received a statement saying I was good and they received all the equipment.  A month later they took another $50 out of my checking for internet service which was totally wrong.  I called TMobile and got the run around than put on hold for over 25 minutes.  I was told that they'd look into it and see about getting it refunded.  Heads up everyone,  don't allow them to pull from your account,  they'll keep taking and trying to get it back will take an act from Congress.  I'll never make this kinda mistake again. Don't believe what your told from them.

If I could give -0 stars I would. Worst company I have ever dealt with. left after over 10 years of being a customer.  the company claimed the broken phone I returned was missing and charged me over 600$, despite paying for insurance over the years. Left the company in December and still receiving bills, with claim that I had 3 lines – I only had one line. They took me to collection and affecting my credit. These seem to be really evil criminals.

So, I've been with T-Mobile since 2007 (they always got the date wrong but that's neither here nor there). They used to be great, USED to. I got the lg v60 two years ago. The phone is great, but the service from T-Mobile has gone all the way down hill since then. I've never had a problem exchanging my phone before. In fact, 
Part 1
I had an issue with the phone not charging correctly. Still do (more on that later). I called customer service/tech to get an exchange. I wanted the phone sent to my house because I was going on vacation. They informed me that they no longer allow that, the phone must be sent to the store. So customer service and they said they would send the phone to the store but x day. Great (not really but, whatever)! I need this before I go to vacation. However, the phone arrived late. By the time I got back from vacation I had an injury, couldn't walk at all. By the time I was mobile, the phone was returned. frustrating yes, can I understand yes, would it have made sense for them to send it to my house? Absolutely (you'll see why shortly)! So I kept a janky phone.
Part 2
I've always loved their service as a whole(in the past). When the Google pixel 6 came out, I went to the store to get a new phone. They took my phone out of the case, this is the first time it has been out of the case since I purchased the phone (important later). They looked at the phone, damage free, and they were about to exchange the phone. They looked at my account, rolled there eyes and said due to my plan (grandfather plan which shows that I have been loyal) I won't be able to change my phone in store. Okay, I'm not upset yet. I call T-Mobile and ask customer service about this. There was a language barrier, ok I get it, I can deal. I explain everything just like I explained it here. The customer service rep kept telling me that due to the pandemic it's hard to make an appointment. Which is not the case, people were just walking in off of the street. After going back and forth for awhile, I hung up out of frustration with full intention to leave the service. I calmed down later and stuck with them.
Part 3.
Here we are, I finally had time out of my schedule, and the consciousness of mind, to call and get this same battery issue resolved. They sent the replacement to the store. I went to pick it up today. Off the bat, the people in the store were rude. The rep didnt look at me. Not because I have a grotesque face, he, Robert, was busy texting on his phone. He grunted at me without looking at me, "did they tell you about the 5.37 fee?" Not hello, not good day, not top of the morning, I'm glad you're not grotesque (customer since 2007). He took my ID and when he had time between text went through the process of starting the exchange. After finally finishing his conversation. He took my phone and removed it from the case (second time the case has been taken off of my phone. The glass on the back had a crack. Not the screen, not anything with anything other than aesthetic value. There was a small crack on the back. He gave it back and said we can't take this. During the exchange process on the phone, they asked is the screen damaged and does the phone power on and off and does it retain power. They didn't ask about the back of the phone. Which I would have discovered when I personally took the cover off for the first time since I had it. Nothing. He told me I had to speak to a manager who would be back in 10 minutes. 5 minutes later, I was told that he would be in in 20 minutes (mind you I'm on lunch break). Whatever I'll call customer service. I had a sweet lady out of Colorado, that answered. She did all she could but ended up giving me the same exact company line. If this was an isolated incident, not even including all the issues that weren't related to this (annual data breaches, constantly having to call them when I come back from out of the country to remind them that my plan allows for calls and text in other countries while in the Americas and they need to fix my bill every.single.time.) I wouldn't be upset. The fact that they give this type of service to a long time customer, customer, not freeloader  (I pay my bills including the insurance and warranty protection because I expect service...which I should probably refunded for). The icing on the cake, I was just trying to find out how to port my phone to another carrier and it took me speaking to 3 or 4 people to get an answer. I wish I could say that the straw that broke the camel's back came 4 issues ago. But all they had to do was give me a response from the first person, and I probably would have said all is forgiven because honestly, who wants to spend a whole day porting a number. Now it's worth it. Stay away from these guys. They are no longer leaders in customer service.
POOR!POOR! Called 5 times about a fraud bill. Total runaround. Sent me to store. Store said nothing they can do.

I have been a Mobile customer for over a year, I have 5 lines for my entire family and I left Verizon due to their service. T-mobile is worst. There is a $75/for international calls to the UK. I don't know anyone in the UK and I do not make any calls internationally. The lady that answered the call was trying to sell me the international plan after she could not find a way to explain the $75 charge. I am in the IT industry and know the VoIP system works, she told me they charge $3.5 per minute of international calling, she ignore my complain and she was asking me to.prove I did not called the UK. Simply unbelievable, RUN from T-mobile, I am start looking for a replacement provider. Their fees are astronomically high and now their selling tactics are basically a ripoff and the FCC should keep and eye. $3.50 per minute in 2022 is plain stealing. Specially if I did not made the call.

Garbage straight trash working for them. Even the supervisors dont give a crap about the fact that they are robbing people with bs add ons to your bill every month. Once your locked in they could careless about you.

Dealing with T mobile is a nightmare! Waste of time on calls with hardly or no resolution. In store people are no better! I am having on going problems with billing to reception problems now going on 10 months. Now with upgrade I am stuck with this  Lousy company for a while. 😡
Worst service. Minimum 2 hours call times, every time I call. Agents don't listen to customers, making us repeat information. Focus is on making sales. Never notate account. Issues never resolved.

This company is totally turning into Sprint which is the worst carrier ever out there has the worst service and I'm done with them I hate to say it I'm going to have to go to Verizon they're the worst as well they don't care none of them do and it's ridiculous they've allowed these guys to consolidate because their service stinks they should have never allowed T-Mobile to combine with Sprint anybody that ever gets involved with this old GTE service inherits their lousy customer service and that's what they have and that's what it continues to be and that's what it will always be the CEO is an idiot total complete idiot not interested at all in knowing what's going on in his business so Good by T-Mobile good riddance

Far and away the worst company I have ever delt with! I have, without exaggeration, spent 3-4, 8 hr days dealing with them, both talking to support and trying to use the web site. I was only attempting to transfer our 4-phone family account over from Sprint and upgrade our phones. Besides having support people that barely speak English, and never listen to why you are calling, they can't help and give conflicting information. The root problem is their software system is designed to increase charges, and it doesn't make it possible for support to help if they even wanted, or even understood why you called in the first place. The cell service is sub-standard but the rates are OK.  Avoid this company at all costs. Oh, BTW the 5G isn't as good as Sprint's 4G, and inoperative along major interstates.

I have been a customer with t-mobile for 9 years now and they just keep getting worse. I got their home internet and used it for around 7 months and sent it back in October and now in March they are trying to charge me a missing router fee of 221 dollars for something I sent them back in October. Not to mention I sent the router back and still got charged for having the home internet for two more months after sending it back. Everyone I talked to has been extremely unhelpful and hasn't even tried to resolve the situation at all. I'm switching carriers as soon as possible

Worked OK for four weeks, only having to reset the router twice.  Then no internet.  2 hours with technical support and a week without internet got me a new router, but after reconnecting for 7 hours, it died again.  They then claimed it was dur to upgrades to my local 5G towers, and would take another week.  That's when I canceled and sent my equipment back.  Good concept,  but they aren't ready to back the weak technology.   Avoid at all costs!

Tmobile has the worst customer service in the WORLD...all agents and sup do is lie. I purchase a Galaxy S22 and the USB cord was missing
I called Tmobile customer service and explained what happen and the first agent lied and told me the USB cord would be expedited and I would get the USB cord in 2 days that was a LIE
I called back and the second agent gave me a fake tracking number and said I would be getting my USB cord in 5,-7 days that was a LIE. Called back and the last agent finally told the truth and said the USB cord had never been shipped out. I was then transferred to a sup by the name of Brittany she told me that they have NO WAY OF COMMUNICATING WITH THE WAREHOUSE-by phone, email, team chat...nothing so she could not tell me why it was taking so long for the WAREHOUSE to ship out the USB CORD
So you mean to tell me that a company as big as Tmobile does not have no way of communicating with the warehouse they depend on to get their products out to their consumer!! 
I have 4 accounts with Tmobile 2 have been with Tmobile for over 16yrs and the other 2 over 20 plus years..I will be taking my business elsewhere.

I have been a t-mobile customer 20 plus years and where I'm living now has the worst service.  I have called numerous times and told that its's the towers and once they merge with Sprint we would get better service.  Well, they have merged and still the same problem.  Today was the last straw.  I was on the phone with medical providers who couldn't hear anything I was saying and we were eventually disconnected.  As much as a pain it will be I'm seriously considering going elsewhere.

They are selling Samsung watches that connect to your phone and DO NOT disclose you can't access Gmail or Watsapp on them. When you realize and come back they charge you $40.00 restocking fee. Beware of this dishonest company!

august 2021, went to local sprint/tmobile store checking on promotions since we had to  upgrade before the 1st of the year due to the new 5g(which is a bunch of bull, we have upgraded phones but still dont get 5g, and still  dropping calls and still no service at work) we also called tmobile customer svs see if maybe they have better promotions than the store, will they did, little cheaper, so we had to turn our 2 samsung glaxay phone in via mail,we would get 400 dollars credit for both phones, we bubble wrapped both phone 4 times and included photos of phone by themselves and also after we bubbled wrapped them we also made copy of photos and send along with the phones,and sent phone thru the local sprint/tmobile store ,well when we recieved our iphones thru the mail, we had to call to get these connected/ etc, they told us the phone were broken etc,will that started everything  no way they were broken ,we have asked them if they received the photos that were in the package no reply, and nothing else was said, recevied the 400 credit,  well after that going thru the contract again, stated it would be a certain amount that everything was good, everything was good till the bill came next month, there it was 10 more dollars added, called them question about bill stated oh 2 dollars added about a tracker,, well they forgot , what ever that tracker is we didnt want that a the begaining, the lady stated we will take it off and you can keep the tracker, we should always know nothing is free, so that bill was correct, well gets even worse, from the date of august 2021 till march or 2022 we have had to call customer service everymonth because there seems to be a charge on bill that shouldnt be, everytime we  would speak to someone they would seem to know our frustration about why we were calling, and would seem to try to help us out, well from each call we would make it would alway be from 1-2 hrs, of being on hold, talking , pausing and than sometimes stated if we can get your number we will continue to work on this and call you back, will only received 2 calls back,  the only thing we ask is to bill us under the terms you stated, not every month for your customer to call question the bill,  well this last call to tmobile/ march 14 2022 it just got to the point, we were all tired /tired  of of them not doing what they had stated they would do , and us spending countless hours on phone trying to get everything straight on bill but that is ok we went ahead and paid off the phones and will never go back to them either at the stores or on line,  unless you want to continue to call them every month and spend 1-3 hrs each time, and complain to 2-3 people everytime you call , thats your decision, but being with sprint for 15 years and them switching over to tmobile,, that was bad,,, and more complaints under the better business bureau also,

I became a full T-Mobile customer migrated from Sprint and went to a retail store because the promotion phone on us with a complete migration from sprint over to T-mobile so we traded in Iphone X's and get new iphone 12minis . They confirmed my bill would be $200 each month since the phone credit would come back to the account each month, well that has not been corrected in 6 months each month. I have talked to all levels of their customer service and no one is getting this fixed. After contacting them each month and them giving me a courteous credit for the phone equipment charge they said I need to go back to the retail store so they can escalate from their end, I have requested a conversation with a VP or President in order to fix this issue, it should be so easy but they are making it so difficult on themselves. T-Mobile failed to honor the "free upgrade" offered in an email and letter. A T-Mobile associate threatened me when I asked for the District Manager's contact information. T-Mobile executives lied to the BBB and the Attorney General Office claiming I accepted a service offer to settle the matter when I did not accept such offer.

I'd leave no stars if I could. In the past 5 months I've had to spend countless hours on the phone listening to T-Mobile lies and fraudulent charges. I tried to turn off the added international package, they said I could keep it for the $60 plan, yet continued to charge me. They said I had 35 g of hotspot data, suddenly it went down to 15 g, then they said they fixed it to 35 g for the same price. There were connectivity problems and they said they'd send a cell spot to improve coverage. I don't have wifi so the device wouldn't work. I called them about this and the representative said oh we have one that you don't need wifi for. They sent another one, and guess what, same device that won't work without an internet connection. I called again and the next representative said oh, the correct one is on backorder, you should get receipt that it is on the way. A month later I called again, this representative then said that they actually do not have a booster device that can work without an internet connection, so many lies and always different information. They sent it free of charge then proceeded to charge me $150 for the device on my next bill. Next they were charging me double the price at $135 a month. They did this last month too (no idea how or why as this was not asked for or approved) and I called in 2 weeks ago to correct that. I see the bill today and they did it again and charged double. I'm tired of the lies and fraudulent charges, this company needs to be held accountable for their actions because as a consumer I'm getting nowhere and literally don't have the time to spend hours and hours each day on the phone with them trying to correct their mistakes. It's causing a lot of emotional distress too. How many times does someone have to ask for a correction to be made? It's so ridiculous!

Where do I start, I received my internet in the middle January by February 1st the signal went from not so good to weak, I've been calling for literally a month for help, no one I mean no one has answers, they keep blaming it on a tour, then it's the same trouble shooting techniques, wipe on, wipe off, shut on shut off, redo your password, all this for 30 days, I call and ask for my rebate of $50.00, well of course still haven't seen that either, I am a disabled person and use my internet for my grandbabies with duo so I can see them and T-mobile is playing with customer's money, they are supposed to be providing a service and they are not, they LIED! ALL LIESSSSSS, NO REBATE, NO INTERNET STREAMING, I watch Netflix and Hulu on my phone every day because I can't stream on the TV due to this weak internet signal, I drive to my local office they tell me to call customer care and ask for a booster, guess what T-mobile says.....YOU CAN'T HAVE ONE, YOU NEED ANOTHER INTERNET SERVICE TO USE OUR FREE BOOSTER 🙄, so now here comes another billing cycle and I haven't enjoyed a full 30 day's of TV streaming but they want another $50.00, I wish I would! This is a case of BBB...... BETTER BUSINESS BUREAU! I refuse to pay another dime! This is ridiculous, I wish I can give them NO STARS!

WORST RECEPTION EVER.. BAD FOR BUSINESS CUSTOMERS!

Best coverage my ass!

I switched to T mobile 4 months ago and also bought a samsung A52 phone from them.  I have absolutely no or minimal reception inside my house. I have to stand outside to be able to call anyone.  Also their Visual Voicemail never works. They said they'd send me a signal booster 3 months ago-never got it.  Probably going to switch again-they totally stink!!!

I asked T-Mobile if I had the lowest rate; that I had found a cheaper rate from Spectrum. The Chat rep said he would get back to me. Every two minutes I got an assurance that he was working on it. Fifteen minutes later, I told him I would only give him two more minutes. Four minutes later I thanked him for helping me decide to leave T-Mobile. He replied that I had their cheapest plan. I go to the Spectrum store. They say I need to pay off my phone. I go to the T-Mobile store and pay it off. I go to the Spectrum store. They say T-Mobile did not release my phone. I return to T-Mobile: Oh, we can't do that here. Call 611. I call. What's this about? There's a long hold time. Want us to call you back when we're ready? Yes. 30 min later they call. And put me on hold for 18 minutes. I then ask them to release my phone. Why. So I can leave this hellish company. What if we give you a discount on your new phone? No. Release my phone. Well, let me pull up your account. Oh the computer is slow today. Let me put you on hold. Ten minutes later. I need to transfer you to a different department. What can we do to keep your business. You have wasted so much of my time, I will NEVER do business with your company again. Finally they release it. I return to Spectrum. What's your T-Mobile account number? I call 611. It starts again. 20 minutes of pain. Why do you need your account number. My computer is slow. Hold. Only my supervisor can give you your account number. How can we keep your business. GUYS, THIS IS A HORRIBLE COMPANY.

I was a happy customer for 3 years. Then they tell me I needed to get new phones for the new 5G system they had. So I go into the store and give them my trusty Samsung galaxy phones. They took my good Samsung phones that I had no problem with, insisted I get new phones, give me 2 JUNK phones for them and when they don't work for over a month tell me I must pay for new ones

I love Mae and Cale.  They run the store and all is good.  I found it odd that two managers walked around the store looking at items and signs when the entire store was packed. Kim and the other male manager were more concerned about a sales rep drink being the counter and a crease in a sign.  I was rather taken back by the way Kim spoke to her employees.  She told me that she was the manager and I thought to myself I wouldn't want to work with or for you.  I mean who redirects an employee with an entire store of customers. Tmobile has the worst customer and tech support there is. I was on the phone with them for six hours then another 2 hours and nothing was resolved. I would never recommended anyone to them.

His name is Junnel and he was wonderful 😊 he has the best customer service skills ever seen before and he went above and beyond my expectations. He's very nice , helpful and patient and friendly and took care of the problem! Thax Junnel and yes you to have a great night

I'm on my daughter's plan so she can build some credit.  The current phone has been giving me a lot of troubles, so I went on the only day off I had in 2 weeks to buy a brand new phone. wish i could give 0 stars. all this company offers is gimmicks without actually providing the service they claim. home internet is a JOKE. told during cancellation after 1 week of not getting signal that i would only be charged for the time i had it. wrong, full charged the $50 and the customer service was rude and unhelpful. stop having your employees lie to your customers, t-mobile. i will forever stay away from you guys and tell others to do the same.

At first, it was rocky, but now it is an excellent gateway.  My speed is around 500 Mbps at 6:00 PM, high traffic time of day. 
T-Mobile bandwidth allows me to work from home and participate in video conferencing meetings.  I added a TPLink mesh router to support the T-Mobile gate through my home.

Not even worth one star. Some days I get no service, people trying to call family members get a message that that line is not in service, some text messages are never received.

I've tried to resolve this with T-Mobile to no avail.

It's really not worth the aggravation. Use anybody but T-Mobile.

My husband and I have been dealing with T mobile for months with no solutions. They say the area I live in has low service, then why promise 5G service if not available. Your company always has excuses for bad service. Honestly a zero star experience. So frustrating!

I have been with them for 16 years
They are getting worst and worst in all categories  i have 7 lines with them  and i am switching to verizon .

T-mobile  is dishonest and corrupt.

T-MOBILE  customer  service: This company  is the worst,  they have been  charging  my account  for extra  lines for 2-3 years! If I could give less than 1 star I would.  I have been with T-Mobile since they were Voicestream, more than 28 years.  Needed to replace a stolen phone.  Angela, the store manager would do absolutely nothing for me to help with the situation, nothing.  Since it was my daughter's phone and needed to be replaced immediately since she is in college, I originally contacted T-Mobile to have the stolen phone disabled and to see if T-Mobile could help me with the new phone.  I was told by the representative that T-Mobile could sell sold me the phone at a lower price (ie: help me as a long time customer) but I would have to wait for it to be mailed to me.  Since I could not wait, the representative told me to go to this corporate owned location and they would help me like he could help me as he made a notation in my account.  But no, Angela offered no help.  More than 28 years of paying this company and no help.  If I was a new customer, I would be given a free phone and all kinds of other things.  So I was forced to buy the phone.  Then the saleswoman, not the store manager, lied to me to get me to buy an overpriced $20 converter plug or I could not charge the phone.  I asked her why?  Shen then informed me that the iPhone has a USB Type C connection.  (I recently purchased an iPad that has a USB C power connection) I said the iPhone 13 doesn't have a lightening connection?  She said yes and that I needed to buy the overpriced $20 converter plug to charge the phone.  At this point, not wanting to give this store another penny, I said no.  When I gave the phone to my daughter, we determined that the iPhone 13 does have a lightening connection and all the cables she has that worked with her stolen phone will work for her new phone.  Do not patronize this T-Mobile location.  Go to a different location.
Horrible service, hate the commercial

I called customer service to try and take of my bill. I was given two options which I could not do as they were too expensive in the long run. The woman on the phone had the nerve to suggest that I get help from my friends and family. It's not possible and she reminded me of how friendless I am. Unprofessional.

The service doesn’t work, you’re basically feeding them money for nothing. No matter where you go, how many bars you have nothing almost ever loads.

We just switched over to T Mobile. It was incredibly  difficult to set up our devices; tablet, phones, and internet 5G Gateway. We could not get anything set up without calling for help. Every time we called, we were transferred at least 4 times. Even though they said they were passing along our issues, we had to explain the problem to each person. We also had to go through multiple verification questions with each person. Every person we talked to tried to upsell us even though we had already agreed to one of the deals. None of these calls took less than an hour. It has been unbelievably complicated and we still don't have our 5G Gateway device set up. Absolutely ridiculous!!

I try not to call T-Mobile's customer service when I don't need to but when I have to call it can be a hit or miss depending on who you talk to. I say it can be a hit or miss depending on if they are friendly depending on if they're going to be helpful to you and your issue is actually going to get resolved. I find out a lot of times if you call and you are frustrated or angry about the service or your bill the representatives aren't able to help you and they don't know how to handle that. All they can do is empathize with you and that can't be enough you want something done about the situation immediately. I will be looking for a new cell phone home and we'll leave T-Mobile cuz I'm just tired of dealing with them.

I had virtually no service the last 6 months with T-Mobile. They made numerous promises and kept charging full price with no issues fixed. I have gone back to Att and service is tons better. Final bill price as quoted by a supervisor was $184 bill arrived being $964. I should sue my data compromised charged for services not provided.
Every time I turn around MORE MONEY. They lied when I purchased device and it's been a battle of funds ever since... DONT USE TMOBLE

I am on a plan with my brother my brother happened to set up the account as the primary account holder he did not pay his bill so my phone stopped working I immediately called gave my brother the $300 that he needed to reinstate the account he did not pay that bill I use my phone number for work one week later my phone stopped working again because he had made the minimum payment okay T-Mobile needs their money I understand so I called and I was told that if I paid off my device is associated to my personal phone number that I would then be in two activations in order to go through the process to activate my own personal account by the way this process is now taking its three hours to get to this point I have collectively been on hold for almost two of those hours after their 11-year employee blatantly lied and told me that if I pay off my $150 that I owe on my device I would then be able to release my phone number and set up my own account this was completely fabricated and it was only an attempt by the representative to receive a payment so that he could receive his Commission when I was transferred to the next apartment they had zero idea of what was going on they didn't told me that unless I paid the $293 outstanding bill that I would actually not be able to process my request and become a new customer T-Mobile is the epitome of pathetic customer service there is actually no customer service there was just people being trained to gather payments there is literally no due diligence process on training your manager reassured me that after horrible process I've been through my number will just be reactivated so that I can wait until Monday when the SIM card they sent me arrives I will set up a prepaid account 12 hours later my phone service again does not work even though I paid $150 as requested by T-Mobile I would tell anyone and everyone that is even considering dealing with T-Mobile to go to any other company a cup on the end of a string would be a better way to communicate with my customers then using T-Mobile I was a 15-year customer T-Mobile has completely drop the ball and has nothing absolutely nothing as far as customer service to offer me just imagine a company being in business that doesn't even allow people with an 800 credit score and a credit card in their hand to set up an account this business should not be in operation the only reason that they survived is because of their Monopoly in the industry T-Mobile is the very worst and I would recommend a gun and one bullet as a better option than choosing to go with this company

I went to add a line for my grandmother. All she needs is the basics, but her phone isn’t compatible with prepaid things like Consumer Cellular. The chat rep seemed questionable, but she did manage to get it set up and get a SIM card mailed to us. She said it would be 10/month additional. Then at the end she surprised me with a pretty big fee for doing it. She said that would be one-time though. But the next month, I checked the app, and my phone bill went up, not by 10, but by 63. Mind I am definitely on the poorer side, so this new bill of 270/month… that’s more than double my allotment for food a month. I called customer service and asked what on earth happened, and he really didn’t have an answer. So basically, the chat rep didn’t know anything and you’re looking at paying roundabouts 100/line/month. So if you want to be ripped off, this is a great company. If you want to not pay in 5 gallons of blood a month, then I suggest you look elsewhere.
